title: Demo Copilot ungrounded: ai-insights-service running with DEMO_MODE=false

title: Risk tab VaR scale mismatch: firm VaR gauge $226K vs sum-of-books $190M (~1000x)
description: Risk tab firm VaR gauge reads ~$226.6K with component breakdown summing to ~$226K, but the Book Contributions table shows 'Sum of books $190.1M' with emerging-markets $89.4M (49.4%) and macro-hedge $87.4M (48.3%). The gauge/component-breakdown and the per-book contribution table are on different scales or scopes (~1000x apart) and cannot both be correct for the same selection. Identify whether the gauge is scoped to a sub-selection while book contributions aggregate the firm, or whether one path has a units bug, and reconcile so a single Risk view is internally consistent.
acceptance_criteria: For a given hierarchy selection the firm VaR gauge, component breakdown total, and sum-of-books are mutually consistent (same scope, same units); add a test asserting gauge total == component breakdown total == reconciled book aggregation within tolerance.
notes: ROOT CAUSE: UI scope mismatch, NOT units. Gauge+ComponentBreakdown+Greeks come from useVaR(bookId) (single book); at Firm level useHierarchySelector returns effectiveBookId=null and App.tsx:373 falls through to a literal book id ('multi-asset', ~226K). Book Contributions table comes from useCrossBookVaR (firm aggregate ~190M). Both internally correct for their scope; shown side-by-side they clash. FIX: in RiskTab.tsx when aggregatedView, drive gauge/breakdown from crossBookResult (it has varValue/expectedShortfall/componentBreakdown), or hide the single-book gauge in aggregated view; guard useVaR from firing with ALL_BOOKS. Files: ui/src/components/RiskTab.tsx:204-222,370-413; VaRDashboard.tsx:183-209; useHierarchySelector.ts:151; App.tsx:373,958.

title: P&L factor attribution: gamma term and unexplained P&L are ~12 billion (units/scaling bug)
description: On the P&L tab (Firm > Multi-Asset, RISK MANAGER) the P&L Waterfall factor attribution shows Gamma = -12,382,262,479 and Unexplained = +12,384,262,713, with percentages like 16,071,186%. The gamma P&L term is off by orders of magnitude, and the unexplained term is the inverse mirror of it, so total nets to a small number (-77,046) but the decomposition is garbage. Likely a units/scaling bug in the gamma P&L contribution (e.g. 0.5*gamma*dS^2 with gamma already per-unit, or dS not scaled), possibly compounded by 'No SOD baseline for today'. Fix the gamma attribution math and verify Delta+Gamma+Vega+Theta+Rho+Unexplained reconciles to total with sane component magnitudes.
acceptance_criteria: P&L waterfall gamma term is within a realistic order of magnitude of delta/vega; unexplained P&L is small relative to total; factor % values are bounded (no >1000% rows); a unit/acceptance test reproduces the bug and passes after the fix.
notes: ROOT CAUSE: PnlComputationService.greekOrFallback() substitutes the VaR-bump gamma (snapshot.gamma ~ -1.0e9 per-fraction^2, from risk-engine greeks.py finite-difference) when no closed-form pricing gamma exists, then PnlAttributionService.kt:37 multiplies it by absolute priceChange^2 -> ~-12.4bn gammaPnl; unexplained = total - explained mirrors it (+12.38bn). PnlAttributionService formula is correct; the INPUT is wrong. FIX: don't fall back to VaR-bump gamma for the Taylor decomposition (PnlComputationService.kt ~77-81) -> gammaPnl=0 when no pricing gamma. Files: risk-orchestrator PnlComputationService.kt:58-89,119-120; PnlAttributionService.kt:36-49. Tests: PnlComputationServiceTest.kt reproducer.

design: Files: demo-orchestrator/src/main/kotlin/com/kinetix/demo/schedule/DefaultPriceBook.kt. May be subsumed by the per-instrument SimulatedPriceBook work, but ship this separately if the per-instrument work is delayed.

title: Demo: replace static DefaultPriceBook with realistic per-instrument prices + random walk
description: The single most credibility-destroying artifact in the demo. DefaultPriceBook currently returns fixed per-asset-class spots ($100 EQUITY, $1.10 FX, $0.05 FIXED_INCOME, $75 COMMODITY, $50 DERIVATIVE). AAPL trades at $100 all day every day. Any front-office PM clocks this as synthetic in under 60 seconds. Replace with a SimulatedPriceBook (in-memory, alongside DefaultPriceBook) seeded with realistic per-instrument levels (AAPL ~185, MSFT ~420, NVDA ~880, EURUSD 1.085, etc.) and a small Gaussian random walk (~±0.3% per tick). Change is confined to demo-orchestrator. After fix: trade quantities make sense, MTM P&L actually moves between ticks.
design: Files: demo-orchestrator/src/main/kotlin/com/kinetix/demo/schedule/DefaultPriceBook.kt, SimulatedTraderJob.kt. Keep DefaultPriceBook as the fallback; wire SimulatedPriceBook behind a config flag (default-on in DEMO_MODE).
acceptance_criteria: Unit tests verify per-instrument seed prices match expected levels; tests verify successive ticks produce different prices within configured drift bounds. Live demo: positions blotter shows realistic prices, unrealized P&L moves between ticks.

title: Phase 3.1: DemoVaRBootstrapJob (TDD)
description:
demo-orchestrator/src/main/kotlin/com/kinetix/demo/schedule/DemoVaRBootstrapJob.kt with runOnce(): BootstrapResult.

Iterates DevDataSeeder.BOOK_HIERARCHY_MAPPINGS.keys (8 books), calls riskOrchestratorClient.calculateVaR(bookId, BOOTSTRAP_PARAMS).

BOOTSTRAP_PARAMS: confidenceLevel=0.95, horizonDays=10, method=PARAMETRIC, valuationDate=today. Overridable via DEMO_BOOTSTRAP_VAR_CONFIDENCE / DEMO_BOOTSTRAP_VAR_HORIZON env vars.

Retry on ConnectException / 5xx with exponential backoff (3 attempts, 500ms/1s/2s). No retry on empty-positions failures (terminal — log and classify as EMPTY).

BootstrapResult shape: data class BootstrapResult(successCount: Int, failureCount: Int, failedBooks: List<String>, durationMillis: Long).

Tests (Kotest FunSpec + MockK):
(a) calls VaR for every seeded book exactly once on first run
(b) idempotency: second runOnce() completes cleanly, same successCount
(c) continues after one book throws, records failure in failedBooks
(d) empty book: 400 'Cannot calculate VaR on empty positions list' → classified EMPTY, not RETRYABLE
(e) retry: ConnectException retried up to 3 times then gives up; transient 5xx retried and eventually succeeds.

notes: ROOT CAUSE: gateway counterparty-risk merge is an unbounded sequential fan-out. CounterpartyRiskRoutes.kt:100-115 collectFirmTradeCounterparties loops every book and calls getTradeHistory(book.id) = FULL unpaginated history (PositionRoutes.kt:308-313), no overall deadline; under demo trade volume this is slow, per-call 5s timeout is swallowed by broad catch. UI authFetch has NO client-side timeout so a slow gateway renders an indefinite 'Loading counterparty exposures...' with 0 counterparties. Data IS seeded (30 counterparties, risk-orchestrator DevDataSeeder.kt:202-238). FIX: (a) UI add AbortController timeout in counterpartyRisk.ts:28-34 -> existing error/empty states resolve; (b) gateway wrap enrichment in withTimeoutOrNull, parallelise per-book async, slim to counterparty-only projection. title: Greeks grid shows near-identical Delta/Gamma across all asset classes (placeholder/synthetic values)
description: Risk tab Greeks grid shows Delta ~= -5.0M and Gamma ~= -1.014bn that are near-identical across EVERY asset class row (Equity, Fixed Income, FX, Derivative). Real books would not have uniform Greeks across asset classes; this looks like a placeholder/synthetic fill or a broadcast bug where one value is repeated. Investigate the asset-class Greek aggregation in the risk path / seed data and ensure per-asset-class Greeks reflect the actual positions.
acceptance_criteria: Asset-class Greeks differ per asset class in a way consistent with the underlying positions (e.g. FX delta != fixed-income delta); a test asserts the rows are not all equal and match summed position-level Greeks.
notes: ROOT CAUSE: real risk-engine bug. calculate_greeks computes per-asset-class Greek as finite diff of portfolio VaR, but _var_value recomputes bumped VaR WITHOUT forwarding correlation_matrix/historical_returns/correlation_method/seed/num_simulations, so bumped runs use the static default model while base_var used the full model. The constant model gap dwarfs the 1% bump -> degenerate uniform Greeks (delta ~ -5.0M, gamma ~ -1.014bn) identical across asset classes. FIX: thread model inputs through _var_value + calculate_greeks and both callers (valuation.py:76-85, server.py:161-168); reuse base seed for MC bumped runs; vega bump must start from base vol provider not DEFAULT_VOLATILITIES. Files: risk-engine/src/kinetix_risk/greeks.py:31-134; valuation.py:60-85; server.py:137-168. Tests: test_greeks.py multi-asset non-uniform assertion.

title: COPILOT_DEMO_MODE_FALLBACK_TOTAL conflates intentional demo mode with Claude-unavailable fallback
description: In ai-insights-service chat/factory.py, build_chat_client() increments COPILOT_DEMO_MODE_FALLBACK_TOTAL on the intentional DEMO_MODE=true path (line ~79) AND on the genuine ClaudeAgentCopilotChatClient construction-failure path (line ~89). A metric named *_FALLBACK_* that also fires when an operator deliberately selects demo mode cannot be used to alert on real degradation (Claude unavailable in prod). Split into two counters: a COPILOT_DEMO_MODE_TOTAL for the intentional path and reserve COPILOT_DEMO_MODE_FALLBACK_TOTAL for the exception branch only.
acceptance_criteria: Intentional DEMO_MODE=true path increments a non-fallback counter; the *_FALLBACK_* counter only increments when ClaudeAgentCopilotChatClient construction throws. Unit test asserts both paths separately.
close_reason: Split intentional demo-mode selection (COPILOT_DEMO_MODE_TOTAL) from Claude-unavailable fallback (COPILOT_DEMO_MODE_FALLBACK_TOTAL) across all three factories with tests. Commit 0453e09d.

title: Intent-routed canned Copilot chat selection (rung 1: replace hash roulette + graceful unmatched)
description:
The canned Copilot chat client (ai-insights-service/src/kinetix_insights/chat/canned.py) selects a transcript via _select_transcript_index = SHA-256(message+page) % len(transcripts). This is content-blind: asking 'what is my VaR?' can return the vol-dislocations or P&L transcript. Any free-typed/off-script question is a 1-in-6 dice roll against the correct topic, and there is no graceful path for questions none of the fixtures cover — it always returns one of the 6 confident answers.

Replace hash bucketing with intent/topic routing so the selected scripted answer matches the question's subject (VaR, P&L, vol, limits, positions, drivers), and add a graceful unmatched response (e.g. 'I can answer about VaR, P&L, vol dislocations, limit breaches, and top positions for this book') instead of a confidently-wrong fixture. Keeps the demo coherent the moment someone goes off the rehearsed script.
design: Keep the CopilotChatClient protocol unchanged. Routing can be keyword/topic match over the fixture corpus (each transcript tagged with the topic/intent it answers) with a deterministic tie-break; embeddings are out of scope for this rung. Determinism must be preserved (sorted corpus, stable selection) so tests and demos replay identically. The unmatched/fallback response is a first-class transcript, not an exception. This rung is selection-only and is orthogonal to rung 2 (kx-fant) which grounds the NUMBERS in real tool results — the two compose: rung 1 picks the right template, rung 2 fills it with live data.
acceptance_criteria: Given the canned chat client, when asked a VaR question it returns the VaR template (not a hash-random one), and similarly for P&L/vol/limits/positions; when asked a question no fixture covers it returns the graceful unmatched response rather than an unrelated confident answer. Selection remains deterministic across runs/machines. Unit tests assert topic->template routing and the unmatched path. No claude CLI spawned on this path.
close_reason: Implemented: intent_router routes canned chat by topic with UNMATCHED fallback; replaces hash roulette. Unit-tested (test_intent_router.py) + canned selection tests updated.

title: Data-grounded canned Copilot chat client (rung 2: real numbers + citations, no Claude)
description:
The canned Copilot chat client (ai-insights-service/src/kinetix_insights/chat/canned.py) replays 6 static fixtures selected by SHA-256(message+page) % 6. The narratives carry HARDCODED numbers (fx-main, $5.2M VaR, +$1.84M P&L, frozen 2026-05-19 timestamps) that do NOT reflect the seeded demo backend. Result: the Copilot 'explains' numbers that contradict the dashboard, which destroys credibility for a risk buyer who cross-checks. This is the deployed showcase behaviour (DEMO_MODE=true on ai-insights-service).

Make the canned chat path 'a bit more real' WITHOUT calling Claude: have the canned client actually invoke the existing MCP tools (get_book_var, get_pnl_attribution, get_vol_surface, get_positions, get_greeks_summary) against the seeded backend via the existing KinetixHttpClient/MCP wiring that the live ClaudeAgent client already uses, then template the REAL tool-result numbers and REAL Citation objects into the scripted narratives. Narrative structure stays scripted/deterministic; the numbers and citations become live and consistent with what is on screen.

Showcases the ADR-0036 MCP-grounding / 'every number is sourced' trust anchor with zero subscription cost, no ~/.claude credential exposure, and no exposure to the claude -p API-reclassification risk.
design: Implement a new client satisfying the existing CopilotChatClient protocol (chat/canned.py) so it slots in via chat/factory.py — do NOT modify the CopilotChatClient protocol. Reuse the MCP tool-call wiring from ClaudeAgentCopilotChatClient rather than re-implementing HTTP calls; the tool layer already stamps X-User-Id/X-User-Books per ADR-0036 service-principal auth. Templates own the prose; tool results own the numbers + Citation list emitted on the terminal SSE frame (mode/model stamps TBD — likely a new 'canned-grounded' mode so it is distinguishable from both 'canned' and 'live'). Selection of which scripted template to use can stay as-is for this issue (hash bucketing) OR be paired with rung-1 intent routing in a separate issue. Frozen as_of timestamps must come from the real tool results, not the fixtures.
acceptance_criteria: Given the seeded demo backend, when the Copilot is asked a scripted question (e.g. VaR explainer, P&L vs yesterday), then every numeric token in the streamed narrative matches the corresponding live MCP tool-result value, and the terminal SSE frame carries Citation objects whose result_value equals the tool result (not the fixture constant). A test asserts the narrative numbers equal the tool-result numbers (the contradiction the current fixtures exhibit). No claude CLI is spawned and ~/.claude is never read on this path. Unit + acceptance coverage per CLAUDE.md; UI Playwright e2e proving the Copilot numbers match the dashboard for at least one book.
close_reason: Implemented for VaR, VaR-drivers, P&L: DataGroundedCannedChatClient pulls live tool numbers+citations, gated by COPILOT_GROUNDED_DEMO. Unit + route-acceptance + UI Playwright coverage. Vol/positions/limits grounding deferred (see follow-up).

title: Decide and document the inter-service trust model (mTLS vs NetworkPolicy)
description: TlsConfig in common/security exists but is disabled; backend services currently trust gateway-forwarded user/role headers, so a compromised pod inside the cluster can impersonate any user by setting those headers. Either enable mTLS service-to-service via the existing TlsConfig hooks, or document the trust boundary explicitly and harden it with Kubernetes NetworkPolicies. Decision blocker before any institutional buyer security review. Acceptance: ADR written, decision implemented, and a NetworkPolicy or mTLS smoke test landed.
notes: Deployment is docker-compose (not K8s) so the NetworkPolicy fallback option is K8s-specific and parked. The mTLS path via the existing common/security/TlsConfig hooks remains the live option for service-to-service trust hardening; the docker-compose equivalent of NetworkPolicies is explicit docker network segmentation.

description: ADR-0017 says the audit trail is hash-chained, but no scheduled job or CLI verifies it end-to-end. First breach discovery will be when compliance asks. Add scripts/support/audit-chain-verify.sh that walks the audit-service hash chain from the most recent entry backwards N entries (default 1000), verifying each link, prints PASS / FAIL with offending entry ID if the chain breaks.
acceptance_criteria: shellcheck scripts/support/audit-chain-verify.sh passes; bash scripts/support/audit-chain-verify.sh --help mentions 'verify'.

title: Script: scripts/support/dlq-inspect.sh
description: alert-rules.yml fires KafkaDlqBacklog and KafkaDlqDepthGrowing correctly, but when the alert fires there is no documented procedure or tool to view the parked messages. Add scripts/support/dlq-inspect.sh <topic> that lists parked messages on a .dlq topic via kcat with headers (failure reason, original offset, retry count); --peek N shows only the first N messages. Inspection only — no replay or delete operations.
acceptance_criteria: shellcheck scripts/support/dlq-inspect.sh passes; bash scripts/support/dlq-inspect.sh --help mentions 'topic'.

title: Script: scripts/support/follow-trade.sh
description: When a trader gives a trade ID, the diagnostic flow today is 5 manual queries across Postgres + audit-service + Loki + Kafka + risk results. Add scripts/support/follow-trade.sh <trade_id> that queries position-service.trades via the gateway, queries audit-service for hash-chained entries referencing the trade, queries Loki for log lines mentioning the trade ID, and prints a single consolidated timeline. Read-only — no Kafka writes.
acceptance_criteria: shellcheck scripts/support/follow-trade.sh passes; bash scripts/support/follow-trade.sh --help mentions 'trade'.

title: Script: scripts/support/price-freshness.sh

Shellcheck-clean, no new dependencies.
acceptance_criteria: shellcheck scripts/support/price-freshness.sh passes; bash scripts/support/price-freshness.sh --all returns at least one line.

title: Dedup LimitType — two enums in position-service
description: position.model.LimitType matches spec; new position.limit.LimitType adds GROSS/NET/BY_COUNTERPARTY/BY_SECTOR with no spec coverage. Check usage of the new one — if unused stub, delete; if used, decide whether to spec it in limits.allium or fold into model.LimitType. See specs/divergences/2026-05-28/group-b-positions-trading.md.
notes:
Investigation completed: scenario (a) — unwired stub.

Findings:
- `position.model.LimitType` (POSITION, NOTIONAL, VAR, CONCENTRATION, ADV_CONCENTRATION, VAR_BUDGET) matches `specs/core.allium:98` referenced from `specs/limits.allium:41`.
- `position.limit.LimitType` (GROSS, NET, BY_COUNTERPARTY, BY_SECTOR) has no spec entry.
- `git grep -n "com.kinetix.position.limit.LimitType"` and `import.*position\.limit\.LimitType` returned zero hits outside the enum's own file and its unit test — no production path invokes it.

Action: deleted enum and its unit test (LimitTypeValidationTest) in branch worktree-agent-af6da628102a8e794. Other files in `position.limit/` package (LimitCheck, LimitTier and their tests) are also unwired stubs but out of scope for kx-hxv0 — separate dead-code question.

`./gradlew :position-service:test` passes. Acceptance tests blocked by missing Docker in this sandbox (Testcontainers), not by this change.
close_reason: Merged to main; see commits c6c89a1f, 83f415b1, 45d1fc76, 4c06a98b, cad567a6.

title: Reconciliation-break PATCH endpoint skips status-transition enforcement
description: Spec forbids transitions like RESOLVED -> OPEN. The new reconciliation-break PATCH handler accepts arbitrary status changes. Apply the same pattern as the alert escalation fix (commit 4679ce0d): currentStatus.canTransitionTo(requestedStatus) -> 409 on failure. Add an acceptance test. See specs/divergences/2026-05-28/group-b-positions-trading.md.
close_reason: Merged to main; see commits c6c89a1f, 83f415b1, 45d1fc76, 4c06a98b, cad567a6.

title: Tenor.kt rejects negative rates despite spec allowing them

Drop the constructor guard; rates are validated at the curve level. Add a unit test for negative-rate construction. See specs/divergences/2026-05-28/group-a-core-market-refdata.md finding A-3.
close_reason: Merged to main; see commits c6c89a1f, 83f415b1, 45d1fc76, 4c06a98b, cad567a6.

title: Demo: default UI theme to dark
description: Per /trader review: every serious institutional trading system ships dark by default (Bloomberg, Eikon, Aladdin, Charles River). Dark canvas is gentler on the eyes for 10+ hour shifts and lets the red/green state changes that matter pop properly. For the demo, dark also signals 'professional tool used by serious people' in the first half-second a viewer sees the screen — free credibility. useTheme already toggles a 'dark' class on html; only the default state needs to flip. Must respect existing users' explicit 'light' preference in localStorage.
acceptance_criteria: useTheme returns isDark=true on first mount with empty localStorage. Existing localStorage='light' is respected and returns isDark=false. Existing localStorage='dark' continues to return isDark=true. Vitest unit tests updated. Manual smoke: open the UI in incognito → loads dark; toggle to light → persists; reload → still light.
close_reason: useTheme defaults to dark; explicit 'light' preference respected; 6 vitest tests cover all paths

title: Demo: add counterparty diversity in DevDataSeeder (multi-counterparty positions, CP risk view)
description: All demo trades currently book against a single implicit counterparty. A credit risk professional reviewing the demo will ask 'how do I see my top-10 counterparty exposures?' and there is nothing to show. Seed 4-6 counterparties in DevDataSeeder (e.g. GS, JPM, MS, CS, BoA, Citi), rotate book trades through them in SimulatedTraderJob, and surface a CP-exposure view in the UI showing concentrations, netting, and CP-by-asset-class breakdown.
design: Files: risk-orchestrator's DevDataSeeder (add CP entities — get architecture sign-off per CLAUDE.md guardrails). demo-orchestrator/src/main/kotlin/com/kinetix/demo/schedule/SimulatedTraderJob.kt + StrategyTradeRequest dto: add counterparty assignment per trade (round-robin or weighted). ui: new CP-exposure tile or subtab. position-service must accept and store counterparty on trade booking.
acceptance_criteria: Acceptance test verifies trades book against 4-6 distinct counterparties. UI CP-exposure view shows non-trivial concentration distribution. Playwright E2E.

title: Demo: bootstrap splash screen — gate UI on /demo/bootstrap-status=READY
description: If a viewer clicks 'Risk' within the first 30-60 seconds of demo startup, they see empty data while DemoVaRBootstrapJob is still IN_PROGRESS. Add a splash overlay in the UI that polls GET /demo/bootstrap-status (already exposed by demo-orchestrator) and shows a 'Demo environment initializing… (N of 8 books ready)' banner until state=READY. Dismiss automatically.
design: Files: ui/src/components/DemoBootstrapGate.tsx (new), wrap Risk / Positions / P&L / Regulatory routes in this gate. Poll every 2s with backoff. Show per-book progress from the bootstrap-status response payload (successCount, failureCount, failedBooks). Only active when demo-orchestrator returns 200 (i.e. running in DEMO_MODE).
acceptance_criteria: Vitest unit test for the gate component. Playwright E2E in ui/e2e/ verifies the splash renders before bootstrap completes and dismisses once state=READY. Acceptance test confirms gate disables itself when demo-orchestrator is not deployed (non-demo environments).

title: Demo: surface a stress-scenario result (e.g. +100bps rates shock) on Risk overview
description: A CRO will ask 'what happens to this portfolio if rates spike 100bps?' within the first few minutes of looking at the Risk view. Risk-engine has Monte Carlo and stress-testing capability. Surface at least one canned stress-scenario result on the Risk overview — even a static daily result — so the CRO sees the full risk story (VaR + limits + stress) and not just the VaR number.
design: Files: risk-orchestrator (verify it can request stress scenarios from risk-engine and persist results), demo-orchestrator/src/main/kotlin/com/kinetix/demo/schedule/ (new StressScenarioSeedJob that fires a canned stress on bootstrap + daily). Canned scenarios: '+100bps parallel shift', 'SPX -5% / vol +5pts', 'USD +3%'. ui/src/components/Risk overview gains a stress-result tile.
acceptance_criteria: Acceptance test verifies a stress-scenario result is captured for each book on bootstrap. Risk overview UI shows the canned scenario card with non-zero P&L impact. Playwright E2E.

title: Demo: add DV01 column to rates-instrument Greek display
description: A fixed-income trader looking at the macro-hedge and fixed-income books expects DV01 (dollar value of a basis point) as the primary rates risk metric, not just generic Delta. Risk-engine almost certainly already computes DV01 (or PV01) for rates instruments; surface it in the P&L Waterfall and the Risk overview for rates books. Without DV01 visible, a rates trader walks away unconvinced even if the underlying calculation is correct.
design: Files: ui/src/components for P&L Waterfall and Risk overview Greek attribution panel (add DV01 column gated on instrument-type=FIXED_INCOME). Backend: confirm risk-engine surfaces DV01 in the snapshot/greek payload; if not, plumb through risk-orchestrator -> gateway.
acceptance_criteria: UI shows DV01 column for FIXED_INCOME positions in P&L Waterfall and Risk hierarchy views. Vitest unit test for the component. Playwright E2E verifies DV01 column renders and contains non-zero values for UST positions after demo seeds trades.

title: Demo: use realistic option prices in DefaultPriceBook (SPX 5000C ~180, VIX 20C realistic)
description: DefaultPriceBook prices all DERIVATIVE instruments at a flat $50. VIX-OPT-20C at $50 implies VIX is at a panic level — a vol PM will spot this immediately. SPX-OPT-5000C should price around $150-200 given a typical vol environment, not $50. Replace the flat DERIVATIVE price with per-instrument lookups consistent with realistic strikes/IVs (depends on the SPX/VIX reference-data wiring in the partner issue). Use risk-engine's pricer (BS or Heston) at startup to compute fair value of each option seed, store as the initial price, and let the random walk handle subsequent moves.
design: Files: demo-orchestrator/src/main/kotlin/com/kinetix/demo/schedule/DefaultPriceBook.kt or its successor SimulatedPriceBook. May depend on the per-instrument prices issue and the SPX/VIX reference-data wiring issue — order via bd dep add.
acceptance_criteria: Unit test verifies SPX-OPT-5000C seed price in [120, 250] range and VIX-OPT-20C in a vol-consistent range (typically $1-$5 for 20-strike calls). A vol PM looking at the derivatives-book blotter does not see flat $50 across all options.
close_reason: BS-derived option seeds shipped — SPX 5000C ~, VIX 20C ~.59, both within acceptance bounds. Verified by OptionPriceSeederTest.

title: Demo: wire SPX/VIX options to reference-data with realistic strikes, expiries, and IV
description: Derivatives book currently uses placeholder instrument IDs (SPX-OPT-5000C, ES-FUT-MAR, VIX-OPT-20C) with no structured detail. A vol PM will ask for strike, expiry, and implied vol within the first 60 seconds of looking at the book. Without reference-data integration, you cannot answer. Wire these instruments to reference-data-service with realistic structured fields (SPX 5000-strike Mar/Jun/Sep expiries, VIX 20-strike, ES Mar future) so the option detail panel shows strike, expiry, IV, and option-greeks attribution that a vol PM would actually recognize.
design: Files: reference-data-service (add option-instrument seed entries — get architecture sign-off per CLAUDE.md guardrails before adding new data shape). risk-engine option-pricing wiring (likely already supports BS/Heston for SPX-style options). demo-orchestrator/src/main/kotlin/com/kinetix/demo/profile/DemoBookProfiles.kt — replace placeholder ids with real reference-data instrument ids.
acceptance_criteria: UI option detail panel shows strike, expiry, IV, delta, gamma, vega for each derivatives-book instrument. Acceptance test verifies reference-data lookup returns structured fields, not raw strings.

title: Demo: add multiple sub-strategies per book in risk-orchestrator DevDataSeeder
description: Currently SimulatedTraderJob falls back to '{bookId}-default' as the only strategy per book (DefaultStrategyIdResolver). No real fund runs a single strategy per book; a buy-side viewer notices the shallow hierarchy in a 15-minute walkthrough. Add 2-3 sub-strategies per book in risk-orchestrator's DevDataSeeder so the portfolio tree drills down like a real fund structure (e.g. equity-growth -> {core, satellite}; derivatives-book -> {vol-arb, directional, hedge}).
design: Files: risk-orchestrator's DevDataSeeder (cross-module change, get architecture sign-off per CLAUDE.md guardrails before adding new data shape). demo-orchestrator/src/main/kotlin/com/kinetix/demo/schedule/SimulatedTraderJob.kt — when picking strategy, randomly choose from book.strategies rather than '{bookId}-default'. demo-orchestrator/src/main/kotlin/com/kinetix/demo/schedule/DefaultStrategyIdResolver.kt — list strategies from server rather than synthesizing a default.
acceptance_criteria: Each demo book has at least 2 strategies in DevDataSeeder. SimulatedTraderJob distributes trades across strategies roughly proportionally. UI portfolio tree shows book -> strategy drill-down with non-trivial position counts under each strategy.

title: Demo: display per-instrument prices in trade blotter UI
description: Even before the random-walk price work lands, the UI should display the seed price per instrument in the trades blotter and positions grid. A viewer who sees 'AAPL 1,850 shares at $100.00' immediately knows something is wrong; a viewer who sees 'AAPL 1,000 shares at $185.20' thinks 'that's close to where AAPL was last year.' The psychology of realistic-looking prices is disproportionate to implementation effort. Blocks on kx-y8g (realistic price work) only for the random-walk view; the column should exist regardless.
design: Files: ui/src/components/TradeBlotter.tsx (price column), ui/src/components/PositionGrid.tsx (last-price column). Surface price from position-service trade record (already persisted) — no new backend work required.
acceptance_criteria: Vitest unit test verifies price column renders. Playwright E2E in ui/e2e/ verifies the column is visible on Trades and Positions tabs and contains non-zero values after the demo seeds trades.

title: Demo: pre-seed a guaranteed limit-breach book for visible early-walkthrough breach event
description: LimitSeedJob sets limits at 80% of current VaR / 70% of delta. With a freshly-bootstrapped portfolio this means breaches only appear after trades accumulate, which can take longer than the available walkthrough time. Designate one book (suggest derivatives-book given its 0.40 trade probability) to have its limit seeded at 50% of initial exposure instead of 80%, guaranteeing a breach within the first 2-3 trade ticks. A visible limit breach in the first few minutes is a powerful credibility signal.
design: Files: demo-orchestrator/src/main/kotlin/com/kinetix/demo/schedule/LimitSeedJob.kt. Add env var DEMO_BREACH_BOOK (default 'derivatives-book') and DEMO_BREACH_VAR_FACTOR (default 0.50). When seeding limits, if the bookId matches DEMO_BREACH_BOOK, use the tighter factor.
acceptance_criteria: Acceptance test: with DEMO_BREACH_BOOK=derivatives-book and trade cadence accelerated, a VAR_95 breach event is recorded for derivatives-book within the first 5 trade ticks of bootstrap completing.

title: Demo: reduce reconciliation break probability from 5% to 0.5-1%
description: SimulatedTraderJob currently posts a deliberately-mismatched prime-broker statement for ~5% of trades (default 0.05). At 90s cadence across 8 books that produces dozens of breaks per trading day, which looks like a system in crisis rather than a system catching edge cases. Real ops break rate target is <0.1%. Reduce DEFAULT_RECONCILIATION_BREAK_PROBABILITY to 0.005-0.01 so we still surface a handful of breaks per day (enough to demo the Reconciliation subtab) but the blotter does not look broken to an ops head.
design: Files: demo-orchestrator/src/main/kotlin/com/kinetix/demo/schedule/SimulatedTraderJob.kt. Env var DEMO_RECONCILIATION_BREAK_PROBABILITY already plumbed; change DEFAULT_RECONCILIATION_BREAK_PROBABILITY constant. Consider biasing breaks toward derivatives-book (highest churn) so one prominent break shows up rather than many spread thin.
acceptance_criteria: Unit test verifies the default is in [0.005, 0.01]. Manual demo run shows 1-3 breaks per simulated trading hour across all books, not dozens.

title: Demo: add POST /demo/trigger-eod admin endpoint for on-demand EOD cycle
description: EodPromotionJob currently fires only at the configured wall-clock close time (default 16:30 UTC). Live demos rarely happen at 16:30 UTC, so the regulatory cycle (the strongest moment of the demo) is unreachable in a 15-minute walkthrough unless you ran the env the night before. Add a POST /demo/trigger-eod admin route that fires the full EodPromotionJob synchronously: VaR snapshot all 8 books, promote each to OFFICIAL_EOD, let risk-orchestrator publish risk.official-eod Kafka events, which EodCycleObserverJob then catches to run backtests and draft regulatory submissions. The endpoint should return 202 with a correlation id once promotion is dispatched.
design: Files: demo-orchestrator/src/main/kotlin/com/kinetix/demo/routes/ (new TriggerEodRoutes.kt), demo-orchestrator/src/main/kotlin/com/kinetix/demo/Application.kt (route registration), reuse EodPromotionJob.run(). Auth: behind DEMO_MODE=true only, no public exposure outside demo deployments. Optional follow-up: a corresponding POST /demo/trigger-sod for symmetry.
acceptance_criteria: Acceptance test fires POST /demo/trigger-eod, verifies all 8 books receive OFFICIAL_EOD promotions, verifies risk.official-eod Kafka events are observed, verifies EodCycleObserverJob runs the backtest and submission draft. Bypasses wall-clock schedule entirely.

title: Live AI in demo: install Claude CLI in ai-insights-service image
description: docker-compose default is DEMO_MODE=false so the factory tries live mode, but the live path fails for two reasons in ai-insights-service/Dockerfile: (1) the runtime image has no `claude` Node CLI (the Python claude-agent-sdk spawns it as a subprocess); (2) the container runs as USER kinetix with no HOME set, while compose mounts ~/.claude:/root/.claude:ro — the SDK reads $HOME/.claude so the mount is effectively ignored. Fix: install Node + @anthropic-ai/claude-code in the runtime image, give the kinetix user a home dir, set HOME=/home/kinetix, and remap the mount to /home/kinetix/.claude in compose and the helm chart.

title: ai-insights.allium spec polish (casing, units, missing wire types)
description: First weed of the new ai-insights spec found: (1) ChatErrorCode enum uses lowercase but wire emits UPPERCASE (e.g. policy_violation -> POLICY_VIOLATION); (2) canned_chat_frame_delay declared as 20.seconds but code is 0.020s (units bug); (3) ChatChunk.tool_calls + ToolCall value type exist on the SSE wire but are absent from spec; (4) five wire-type fields declared as String with @guidance documenting structured shapes (page_context, Citation.params, Citation.result_value). All spec-side fixes. See specs/divergences/2026-05-28/group-f-ai-insights.md.

title: scenarios.allium SavedScenario missing id field
description: SavedScenario entity in scenarios.allium has no id field; code populates an id everywhere. Likely dropped when migrating from GovernedStressScenario. Add id: ScenarioId. See specs/divergences/2026-05-28/group-e-scenarios-governance.md finding E-S2C-19.

title: audit.allium missing AuditEventType.risk_calculation_failed
description: Code emits AuditEventType.RISK_CALCULATION_FAILED (commit ef93c408 on 2026-05-21) but audit.allium enum doesn't list it and no rule covers it. Add the enum member and a producing rule reference. See specs/divergences/2026-05-28/group-e-scenarios-governance.md finding E-S2C-18.

title: Demo: handle market holidays in SimulatedTraderJob (no trading on US holidays)
description: SimulatedTraderJob already skips weekends but does not pause for market holidays (Thanksgiving, Christmas, July 4, etc.). Demoing on a US holiday with trades printing is a minor but visible authenticity gap. Add a US holiday calendar check alongside the existing weekday check.
design: Files: demo-orchestrator/src/main/kotlin/com/kinetix/demo/schedule/SimulatedTraderJob.kt and SchedulingHelpers.kt. Use a simple static set of US market holidays for the current/next year (no external API). If a holiday calendar utility already exists in common/ or reference-data, prefer reusing it.
acceptance_criteria: Unit test verifies SimulatedTraderJob no-ops on 2026-07-03 (observed July 4), 2026-11-26 (Thanksgiving), 2026-12-25, etc. Production calendar covers at least 12 months forward.

title: Demo: document DEMO_TRADE_CADENCE_SECONDS=30 as the live-demo setting
description: Trade cadence defaults to 90 seconds, which is too slow for a live walkthrough where you want visible blotter activity within 2-3 minutes. The env var DEMO_TRADE_CADENCE_SECONDS already exists in DemoConfig — document 30 as the recommended live-demo setting and 90 as the recommended background-running setting. Add to demo runbook / README.
design: Files: deploy/ (compose or helm values for live-demo profile) — keep 90s as the default for normal runs, override to 30s in a 'live-demo' profile. demo-orchestrator/README.md or a docs/demo/runbook.md if appropriate. Surface in the /demo skill output too.
acceptance_criteria: Documented in the demo runbook / readme that 30 = live-demo, 90 = background. Live-demo compose profile sets DEMO_TRADE_CADENCE_SECONDS=30.

title: Periodic cleanup of accumulated Claude agent worktrees (280 admin entries, 98 on-disk dirs)
description: The repo accumulated ~98 .claude/worktrees/agent-* directories and 280 git worktree admin entries; 47 are locked to live agent PIDs, 3 dangle pointing at a sibling clone (IdeaProjects/kinetix, not kinetixlk) so 'git worktree prune' leaves them. The dirs no longer pollute git status (now gitignored, commit ff8f5534) but the metadata bloat slows git worktree ops. Establish a periodic cleanup: git worktree prune for genuinely stale entries, git worktree remove for clean finished ones, and decide whether the cross-repo dangling entries should be force-removed.
acceptance_criteria: A documented/scripted cleanup reduces git worktree admin entries to active+recent only, without removing any worktree locked to a live PID.
close_reason: Added scripts/prune-agent-worktrees.sh (safe: skips locked/live + dirty worktrees, preserves branches). Removed the two integrated review worktrees. Commit 66960600.

title: Canned grounded Copilot: multi-turn follow-ups route fresh with no conversation memory
description: DataGroundedCannedChatClient.chat() discards history (del history, data_grounded_canned.py:156). A follow-up like 'and what is driving it?' is re-routed by intent_router from scratch with no memory of the prior turn, so it cannot resolve pronouns/context across turns. Acceptable for the current scripted canned scope; tracking as a known limitation. Revisit if the grounded canned path needs conversational continuity (e.g. carry last book_id/topic into route_intent).
acceptance_criteria: Either: a follow-up referencing the prior turn resolves to the same book/topic; or a deliberate decision is recorded that the canned path stays stateless and the issue is closed with that rationale.
notes: Decision (2026-06-02): the canned grounded/demo chat path stays intentionally STATELESS. route_intent (intent_router.py) is documented as a pure function of (message, page) with no clock/randomness/I/O so demos replay identically. A follow-up like 'what is driving it?' carries no 'var' token, so it routes to UNMATCHED or the page fallback (plain VAR) rather than VAR_DRIVERS — confirmed limitation. Threading conversation history into routing would contradict that purity principle. Multi-turn continuity belongs to the LIVE Claude path (ClaudeAgentCopilotChatClient), which already receives history; the canned demo path deliberately does not. Closing as by-design per this issue's acceptance criteria ('a deliberate decision is recorded that the canned path stays stateless'). Reopen if the demo needs conversational follow-ups.
close_reason: By design: canned demo chat path is intentionally stateless; multi-turn continuity is the live Claude path's responsibility. Decision recorded in notes.
